purpose.summary: This program interacts with an IMS database to retrieve and update pending authorization details. It retrieves authorization summary and detail segments, and allows for marking authorizations as fraudulent. The program uses CICS SYNCPOINT and ROLLBACK commands for transaction management.
purpose.business_context: This program likely supports fraud detection and prevention by allowing users to flag suspicious authorizations.

inputs.name: PAUTSUM0
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Summary segment, containing account ID and authorization key.

inputs.name: PAUTDTL1
inputs.description: Pending Authorization Details segment, containing authorization details and fraud indicators.

inputs.name: WS-ACCT-ID
inputs.description: Account ID used to retrieve authorization summary.

inputs.name: WS-AUTH-KEY
inputs.description: Authorization key used to retrieve authorization details.

inputs.name: WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD
inputs.description: Fraud authorization record used to update the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment.

outputs.name: PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS
outputs.description: Updated Pending Authorization Details segment, potentially marked as fraudulent.

outputs.name: WS-MESSAGE
outputs.description: Message displayed on the CICS screen to the user.

called_programs.purpose: Displays an error message on the CICS screen.

business_rules.description: If the IMS return code indicates an error while reading authorization summary or details, set the error flag and display an error message.
business_rules.logic_summary: Evaluates DIBSTAT after each DLI call and performs error handling if the status is not STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB.

business_rules.description: If the IMS return code indicates an error while updating authorization details, perform a CICS rollback.
business_rules.logic_summary: Evaluates DIBSTAT after the REPL call and performs ROLL-BACK if the status is not STATUS-OK.
conditions: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK after REPL call

copybooks_used.purpose: Defines the structure of the PAUTSUM0 IMS segment.
Defines the structure of the PAUTDTL1 IMS segment.

paragraphs.purpose: This paragraph retrieves pending authorization records from an IMS database. It first calls SCHEDULE-PSB to schedule the PSB. It then moves the account ID (WS-ACCT-ID) and authorization key (WS-AUTH-KEY) to the corresponding fields in the PAUTSUM0 segment. A 'Get Unique' (GU) DLI call is issued to retrieve the authorization summary segment (PAUTSUM0) based on the account ID. If successful, a 'Get Next' (GNP) DLI call is issued to retrieve the authorization details segment (PAUTDTL1) based on the authorization key. The IMS return code (DIBSTAT) is evaluated, and if an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed via SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. If the segments are found, flags are set accordingly.

This paragraph retrieves the next pending authorization detail record from the IMS database. It issues a 'Get Next' (GNP) DLI call to retrieve the next authorization details segment (PAUTDTL1). The IMS return code (DIBSTAT) is evaluated, and if an error occurs, an error message is constructed and displayed via SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN. The paragraph sets AUTHS-NOT-EOF or AUTHS-EOF based on the DIBSTAT value. This paragraph assumes that the PSB has already been scheduled.

This paragraph updates the pending authorization details segment (PAUTDTL1) in the IMS database. It moves the fraud authorization record (WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD) to the PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS segment. A 'Replace' (REPL) DLI call is issued to update the segment in the database. The IMS return code (DIBSTAT) is evaluated. If the update is successful, a CICS SYNCPOINT is taken to commit the changes. If the update fails, a CICS ROLL-BACK is performed to undo any changes, an error message is constructed, and the SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN paragraph is called to display the error. The paragraph also displays 'AUTH FRAUD REMOVED...' or 'AUTH MARKED FRAUD...' based on PA-FRAUD-REMOVED.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT command to commit the changes made to the database. This ensures that the updates are permanently saved. It is called after a successful update of the authorization details.

This paragraph issues a CICS SYNCPOINT ROLLBACK command to undo any changes made to the database. This is performed when an error occurs during the update of the authorization details, ensuring data consistency. It is called by UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS when the DLI REPL call fails.

This paragraph schedules the Program Specification Block (PSB) required for accessing the IMS database. It issues a 'Schedule' (SCHD) DLI call with the PSB name (PSB-NAME) and the NODHABEND option. If the PSB has been scheduled more than once (PSB-SCHEDULED-MORE-THAN-ONCE), it first terminates the existing schedule and then schedules it again. If scheduling is successful, the IMS-PSB-SCHD flag is set to TRUE. If an error occurs during scheduling, an error message is constructed and displayed via SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB during READ-AUTH-RECORD
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK, SEGMENT-NOT-FOUND, or END-OF-DB during READ-NEXT-AUTH-RECORD
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK during UPDATE-AUTH-DETAILS
error_handling.action: Perform ROLL-BACK, set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.

error_handling.condition: DIBSTAT not equal to STATUS-OK during SCHEDULE-PSB
error_handling.action: Set WS-ERR-FLG to 'Y', construct an error message, and call SEND-AUTHVIEW-SCREEN.
Terminate the PSB and schedule it again.

open_questions.context: The code moves WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD to PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS, but the structure of WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD is not defined in the provided code snippet.
open_questions.suggestion: Examine the copybook definitions or other parts of the program to determine the fields within WS-FRAUD-AUTH-RECORD.
